name: Labor Camps
description:
Labor Camps — Construction Site Camps for 50 to 500 Workers, Ready in 10 Days

A labor camp is what most Indian site engineers actually call the worker accommodation setup they put up at the start of a new construction or infrastructure project. SAMAN supplies standard labor camps for 50 to 500 workers from our manufacturing facilities in Bangalore and Greater Noida, with pan-India delivery and a 10-day site-ready target for most standard configurations.

Why Indian Site Engineers Use the Term "Labor Camp"

SAMAN labor camp deployed alongside an NHAI highway construction corridor — typical 100 to 300 worker configuration.

"Labor camp" is the procurement term used across Indian highway, dam, power, and factory project sites. It refers to the active worker accommodation deployed at the start of a project — short to medium duration, sized to the workforce, dismantled when work ends.

In our labor colony range, three closely related products serve different needs: a colony is the long-stay infrastructure version with worker recreation space, labor hutments are the basic worker sleeping units, and a labor camp is the camp-as-a-whole deployment.

What's Inside a Standard Labor Camp Setup

A standard SAMAN labor camp deployment ships with four functional elements:

  • Dormitory blocks for worker sleeping accommodation
  • Washroom and toilet blocks sized to the worker count per BOCW Act ratios
  • Canteen unit with cooking provisions
  • Supervisor quarter with separate sleeping and office space

Walls are insulated PUF sandwich panels on an MS frame, with PPGI roofing. Each block ships pre-finished — wiring conduits, lighting points, doors, windows, and ventilation openings are fitted before the unit leaves the line.

SAMAN labor camp deployed at an Indian metro rail construction project — dormitory and canteen blocks visible in one frame.

For buyers who need a unit-by-unit specification of dormitory, washroom, and canteen blocks, see our labor accommodations subpage.

Sizing Your Labor Camp — From 50 to 500 Workers

The camp size you order is driven by your worker count, available land, and whether your site can accommodate single-storey or stacked dormitory placement.

Camp sizing below covers the four standard inclusions — dormitory, washroom, canteen, and supervisor quarter. Full colony layouts add a medical room provision and dedicated supervisor block at the 200+ worker tiers; see our labor colony hub for those additions.

Worker count Dormitory units Washroom blocks Canteen Approx. land footprint Storey
50 5–7 2 1 30m × 20m Single
100 10–12 3–4 1 45m × 25m Single
200 20–25 6–7 2 75m × 35m Single or double
300–500 30–55 8–10 2–3 80m × 45m Double (stacked)

The numbers above are first-pass planning sizes. The exact camp layout for your project is locked at the pre-order site survey, where we confirm dormitory placement, washroom orientation, and dispatch sequence.

SAMAN labor camp deployed at a bridge construction site — single-row layout for the 100 to 200 worker tier.

Site-Ready in 10 Days — The Camp Deployment Timeline

For a standard 100 to 200 worker camp on accessible land, the camp goes from confirmed order to handover in 10 working days:

  • Days 1 to 3: Site preparation completes, ground levelling is signed off, and the first dispatch — dormitory and sanitation units — lands on site.
  • Days 4 to 7: Bolt-and-panel assembly of all units. Second batch (canteen, supervisor quarter) arrives and is placed.
  • Days 8 to 10: Electrical hookup to your site connection, water line connection, doors and windows fitment check, and handover.

For 300 to 500 worker camps, the timeline extends to 14 to 16 days because dispatch is phased across three to four truck batches. Phased worker arrival aligned to phased camp completion is standard practice for that tier.

Match the Camp to Your Project Duration

SAMAN labor camp mid-deployment at a thermal power plant project — crane placing units across a 10-day site-ready window.

Your project's expected duration is the cleanest filter for the right product choice in this cluster:

Project duration Recommended product Why
Under 6 months Standard labor camp (this page) Basic fitout is sufficient; rental usually preferred
6 to 12 months Standard labor camp (this page) Purchase becomes cost-effective; standard fitout fits
12 to 18 months Prefab labor camp (link below) Factory-prefabricated units give better fitout consistency
18+ months Prefab labour colony Full colony with recreation space, washable finishes, monsoon-rated roof seals

For factory-prefabricated camp delivery with quality-checked units across all batches, our prefab labor camps subpage covers that configuration.

What Drives the Camp Cost — Not a Single Per-Sq-Ft Number

SAMAN labor camp on factory land — typical 6 to 12 month project duration with a compact L-shaped layout.

Indian B2B price listings for labor camps span roughly Rs 650 to Rs 2,000 per sq ft across suppliers. The spread is real, and it is explained by five factors:

  • Worker count — economies of scale apply above 100 workers
  • Single or double storey — stacked dormitories raise per-sq-ft cost but reduce land footprint
  • Site accessibility — remote or no-crane sites need lighter unit formats and add freight cost
  • Project duration — longer duration justifies upgraded fitout
  • Fitout level — basic vs upgraded interior, sanitation grade, electrical specification

We quote against your specific inputs rather than a generic per-sq-ft rate.

Tell Us These Five Things and We'll Quote in 24 Hours

A camp quote turns around in 24 working hours once we have these five details from your side:

  • Total workers on site and whether the project runs single or two-shift
  • Expected project length, stated in months
  • Available land — approximate dimensions and ground type at the proposed camp position
  • State and nearest road for the truck convoy, plus any access restrictions
  • Date you need the camp commissioned and ready for worker move-in

Back from us: camp layout sketch with unit positions, full unit count breakdown, batched delivery schedule, and itemised pricing — purchase or rental where the project duration supports it.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937

Frequently Asked Questions

Is a labor camp the same as a labor colony?

No. A labor camp is the standard active-project deployment — sized to the workforce, intended for short to medium duration, dismantled at project end. A labor colony is the long-stay infrastructure version with worker recreation space, designed for 18+ month projects. Same cluster, different products.

Does a labor camp setup include washroom, toilet, and canteen blocks, or are these billed separately?

A standard SAMAN labor camp order is a bundled deployment — dormitories, washroom and toilet blocks, canteen unit, and supervisor quarter are all included in the camp quote. Itemised breakdowns are available on request for tender or audit purposes.

Should I buy or rent a labor camp for a project under 12 months?

Rental usually makes more sense for projects under 8 months. Between 8 and 12 months, the answer depends on whether your next project is back-to-back — if redeployment is likely within 6 months of dismantle, purchase becomes economically stronger.

Can SAMAN install a labor camp on uneven or sloping ground?

Minor undulation up to about 5° is handled with adjustable footing blocks during placement. Slopes above 5° need pre-levelling or stepped placement, which is confirmed during the site assessment before dispatch. Heavy gradient sites may need foundation pads.

Manufactured at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities.

Pan-India delivery in 7 to 21 working days from confirmed order. For accommodation under 50 workers, our prefabricated houses range covers bunkhouse and site accommodation units.

A standard labor camp deployment for Indian construction and infrastructure projects. Dormitory, washroom, canteen, and supervisor blocks bundled in one camp order. Pan-India delivery from Bangalore and Greater Noida.

50–500 workers
